data.title: The White House held its third closed-door meeting on stablecoins, with crypto executives saying "there is still room for further development."
data.description: The White House held its third closed-door meeting on Thursday, inviting representatives from the crypto industry and banking groups to discuss stablecoin rewards mechanisms. The meeting, which lasted several hours, was described by attendees as a "constructive dialogue," but no final compromise was reached. Ji Hun Kim, CEO of the Crypto Council for Innovation, stated that the meeting further advanced the framework based on previous discussions, aiming to enhance U.S. competitiveness while protecting consumers. Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal also described the discussions as "cooperative and constructive," hinting that further negotiations would continue. Stablecoin holding rewards mechanisms are a key point of contention in current crypto market structure legislation. Sources familiar with the matter revealed that the White House proposed some principles allowing companies to offer rewards based on specific activities or account balances, but banks remain cautious. Whether a second round of meetings will be held is yet to be determined. Participating organizations included Ripple, the Blockchain Association, the Crypto Council for Innovation, and several major banking associations.

data.title: Eric Trump: Bitcoin will reach $1 million, says he's "never been so bullish."
data.description: At the World Liberty Financial forum in Mar-a-Lago, Eric Trump reiterated his optimistic outlook on Bitcoin's long-term price, stating that Bitcoin "will eventually reach $1 million" and that he has "never been more bullish on cryptocurrency." In an interview with CNBC, Trump pointed out that Bitcoin has seen an average annual increase of about 70% over the past decade, calling it "one of the best-performing asset classes of the last ten years." He stated that while price volatility exists, it is the price that high-growth assets must bear. Currently, Bitcoin's price is hovering around $67,000, a significant drop from its high of $126,000 reached in October 2025. Trump had previously predicted that Bitcoin would reach $175,000 by the end of 2025, with a long-term target of $1 million. This statement comes as the Trump family deepens its involvement in the crypto space, with World Liberty Financial considered one of its core crypto projects.

data.title: A Federal Reserve research paper states that market forecasting has significant analytical value.
data.description: A recent paper published by researchers at the Federal Reserve highly praises the role of forecasting markets in economic analysis, specifically highlighting the performance of the forecasting platform Kalshi. The report points out that Kalshi's forecasts of the federal funds rate and the U.S. Consumer Price Index (CPI) statistically outperform federal funds futures and professional economist forecasts, providing continuously updated complete probability distributions rather than traditional discontinuous point forecasts. The study also argues that forecasting markets provide unique market-based distribution data in areas such as GDP growth, core inflation, unemployment rate, and non-farm payrolls, variables for which comparable market pricing tools were previously lacking. Since 2022, Kalshi's forecasts of federal funds rate meetings have been perfectly consistent with actual results. The report notes that a major advantage of forecasting markets lies in the participation of retail investors, distinguishing them from traditional institutionally dominated financial markets and giving them unique value in information integration and real-time reflection of market expectations.

data.title: Light Earnings Day Expected to Conclude the Week
data.description: The week is set to end with a light earnings schedule, with companies Lamar Advertising Company ($LAMR) and PPL Corporation ($PPL) reporting before the market opens. Bespoke Investment Group posted on X. Investors are keeping an eye on these reports as they assess the financial health and future prospects of these companies. The earnings reports are anticipated to provide insights into the advertising and utility sectors, respectively. Market participants are particularly interested in how these companies have navigated recent economic challenges and what their outlooks suggest for the coming months.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Claims Success in Addressing Cost-of-Living Concerns
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced a victory in tackling cost-of-living issues, emphasizing a shift in strategy that downplays challenges within his economic policies. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Trump's focus on achievements in job creation and other economic indicators. The President's remarks suggest a confident stance on the administration's handling of economic matters, despite ongoing debates about the effectiveness of his policies. Trump's approach aims to highlight positive outcomes while addressing public concerns over living expenses.

data.title: Santiment Integrates API with Claude Build
data.description: Santiment posted on X about their excitement regarding the integration of their API with the Claude build. The company expressed enthusiasm for the potential capabilities and developments that this integration could bring. Santiment plans to closely monitor the progress and outcomes of this collaboration.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Daly Highlights Challenges in Achieving Inflation Goals
data.description: Federal Reserve official Daly has noted the difficulties in reaching inflation targets due to various shocks, including tariffs. According to ChainCatcher, these challenges have persisted in the final steps of achieving the desired inflation rate.

data.title: Consumer Staples Outperform Consumer Discretionary Over the Past Year
data.description: Consumer Staples have outpaced Consumer Discretionary in the past year, marking a notable shift in market dynamics. Bespoke Investment Group posted on X that Consumer Staples recorded a 7.6% increase, while Consumer Discretionary saw a 2.6% rise. This performance highlights the resilience of Consumer Staples in a competitive market environment.

data.title: Consumer Discretionary Sector Experiences Decline Amid Amazon and Tesla Losses
data.description: The Consumer Discretionary sector has seen a 5% decrease since the beginning of the year, primarily due to declines in its two largest constituents, Amazon and Tesla. Bespoke Investment Group posted on X. Amazon's shares have dropped by 11.5%, while Tesla's have fallen by 8.5%. Despite these losses, 10 of the 15 largest stocks in the sector have recorded gains this year.

data.title: Dollar Steady as U.S. Labor Market Shows Strength
data.description:
The dollar remained stable as recent data indicated a robust U.S. labor market, providing the Federal Reserve with room to maintain interest rates in the short term. According to RTHK, the dollar index was around 97.9 at the close of trading in New York, marking a 0.2% increase and extending gains for four consecutive trading days. The euro fell 0.1% against the dollar, trading at $1.1768, following reports that European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde might leave before her eight-year term ends. The British pound dropped approximately 0.3% against the dollar, trading at $1.3456, while the dollar rose 0.1% against the yen, reaching 155.04 yen.

Last week, the number of initial unemployment claims in the U.S. decreased by 23,000 to 206,000, which was lower than the expected 225,000. Minneapolis Federal Reserve Bank President Neel Kashkari stated that the labor market remains resilient, and the Federal Reserve is close to achieving its dual goals of full employment and stable prices. Interest rate futures suggest that the market sees less than a 50% chance of a rate cut of at least 0.25% before the Federal Reserve's June meeting.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Daly Notes Improvement in Labor Market After Rate Cut
data.description: The Federal Reserve's Daly has observed improvements in the labor market following a 75 basis point interest rate cut. According to ChainCatcher, this development comes as part of the central bank's ongoing efforts to stabilize the economy. The rate cut aims to support economic growth and address challenges in the labor sector.

data.title: Venezuela Raises Fuel Prices Amid Oil Industry Revitalization
data.description: Venezuela has initiated an increase in fuel prices at gasoline stations in Caracas as part of its strategy to generate new revenue. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the country's efforts to reopen its oil industry to foreign investment and enhance production. This move comes as Venezuela aims to revitalize its economy by attracting international investors and boosting its oil output.

data.title: Pop Culture Icons Propel Billionaire Growth in Japan
data.description: Japan's pop culture phenomena, including Hello Kitty and Demon Slayer, are contributing to the rise of billionaires in the country. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how these cultural icons are driving significant economic growth. The success of these franchises has led to substantial financial gains for their creators and stakeholders, reflecting the global appeal and commercial potential of Japanese pop culture. As these brands continue to expand their reach, they are playing a pivotal role in shaping Japan's economic landscape, underscoring the lucrative nature of entertainment and media industries.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Europe to Collaborate on Affordable Air Defense Systems
data.description: Europe's top five military spenders are planning to collaborate on developing cost-effective air defense weapons, drawing insights from the conflict in Ukraine. Bloomberg posted on X, revealing a draft statement and insights from individuals familiar with the initiative. This joint effort aims to enhance defense capabilities by leveraging battlefield experiences and optimizing resources. The collaboration underscores the strategic importance of air defense in modern warfare, as European nations seek to bolster their security infrastructure in response to evolving threats.

data.title: Veteran Designer Criticizes Generative AI in Creative Processes
data.description: Jervis Johnson, a seasoned designer of Warhammer 40,000, has expressed strong criticism of generative AI, describing it as the 'asbestos of the internet' due to its potential long-term detrimental effects. According to NS3.AI, Johnson supports Games Workshop's decision to prohibit AI-generated content in their creative processes, emphasizing the importance of maintaining high-quality, human-driven design. He argues that AI might promote creative laziness and bypass the rigorous human effort essential for genuine innovation.

data.title: U.S. Makes Partial Payment to UN Amid Funding Concerns
data.description: The United States has recently made a partial payment to the United Nations, addressing only a small portion of its approximately $2 billion debt to the organization. Bloomberg posted on X that this payment comes as the UN faces a significant funding crisis. The financial shortfall has raised concerns about the organization's ability to maintain its operations and fulfill its global commitments. The UN has been urging member states to meet their financial obligations to ensure the continuity of its programs and initiatives. The U.S. payment, while a step forward, highlights the ongoing challenges the UN faces in securing adequate funding from its members.

data.title: Actor T.J. Miller Discusses AI Impact on Performance Industry at ETH Denver
data.description:
Actor T.J. Miller, during his appearance at ETH Denver, shared his perspective on the potential impact of artificial intelligence on the performance industry. According to NS3.AI, Miller expressed minimal concern about AI replacing human performers, despite the growing apprehension within the industry regarding automation. He acknowledged the swift advancements in AI technology but advised against completely relinquishing control of life to AI systems.

At the same event, blockchain developers emphasized the benefits of utilizing decentralized infrastructure to enhance AI capabilities in trading and identity management. They discussed how blockchain technology could empower AI applications, potentially leading to more secure and efficient processes in these areas.

data.title: Anthropic Services Projected to Impact Business Budgets Significantly
data.description: The Long View, institutional investor, posted on X that businesses may soon face substantial costs for Anthropic services, with projections suggesting expenses exceeding $1,000 per user monthly. The investor emphasized the value of these services, noting their transformative impact on companies. Planning for the upcoming year, the investor has allocated funds for over 200 employees, expressing willingness to pay the anticipated costs due to the significant and lasting changes these services bring to their organization.

data.title: Polish Foreign Minister Criticizes Nuclear Weapon Proposal
data.description: Polish Foreign Minister Radoslaw Sikorski has publicly criticized President Karol Nawrocki for suggesting that Poland should acquire nuclear weapons. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Sikorski's remarks, which labeled the proposal as 'irresponsible and harmful.' Sikorski's comments reflect concerns over the potential implications of such a move on regional stability and international relations.

data.title: Oil Prices Rise as Dow Drops Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: Oil prices increased while the Dow Jones Industrial Average declined by over 260 points on Thursday. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting that investors are closely watching the U.S. military buildup in the Middle East. The geopolitical tensions in the region have contributed to fluctuations in the market, affecting investor sentiment and leading to a cautious approach in trading. As the situation develops, market participants remain vigilant, assessing the potential impact on global oil supply and broader economic implications.
